body:
How to File a Grievance

A grievance must be filed in writing within 10 working days of the event or the date the member became aware of it. Step 1 is the informal discussion between the steward and the immediate supervisor. If unresolved, the steward submits the written Step 2 grievance to the labor relations rep.

Always record the contract article allegedly violated, the remedy sought, and the dates of every contact. Keep a copy in the member file.
title: How to File a Grievance

body:
Grievance Timeline Reference

Step 1 informal: 10 working days to raise. Step 2 written: 10 working days after Step 1 answer. Step 3 to the Local president: 15 working days. Demand for arbitration must be served within 30 calendar days of the Step 3 denial.

Missing a deadline can waive the grievance, so stewards should calendar every date immediately and confirm receipt with the company.
title: Grievance Timeline Reference

body:
2027 Bargaining Priorities

The bargaining committee voted to prioritize wage catch-up after two flat years, restoration of the cost-of-living allowance, and stronger language on subcontracting. Healthcare premium share is the top non-economic concern raised in the member survey.

Andre is compiling shop-floor input by department before our next prep session.
title: 2027 Bargaining Priorities

body:
Member Survey Results — Spring

We collected 412 surveys out of roughly 600 members. Top three issues: wages (78 percent), mandatory overtime (61 percent), and healthcare costs (54 percent). Skilled trades flagged tool allowance and apprenticeship ratios.

Raw tallies by department are attached for the bargaining committee.
title: Member Survey Results — Spring

body:
Arbitration Award: Overtime Bypass 2023

Arbitrator Hollis sustained the grievance and ordered the company to make the senior employee whole for hours worked by a junior employee out of seniority. The award confirmed that Article 12 overtime equalization is mandatory, not discretionary.

Use this award as precedent when management claims operational need overrides the OT list.
title: Arbitration Award: Overtime Bypass 2023

body:
Hearing Preparation Checklist

Before any arbitration hearing: assemble the grievance file in chronological order, prepare a witness list with contact info, draft direct-examination questions, and identify every contract article in dispute. Confirm the grievant is available and prepped on what to expect.

Carla coordinates room booking and the court reporter when needed.
title: Hearing Preparation Checklist

body:
Lockout/Tagout Procedure

No member services or repairs energized equipment without applying a personal lock and tag. Each member places their own lock; locks are never removed by anyone but the person who applied them. Report any machine found running without proper guarding to the safety committee at once.

Refusal of unsafe work is protected under Article 18 and OSHA.
title: Lockout/Tagout Procedure

body:
Reporting a Workplace Injury

Members must report any injury to their supervisor and the medical station the same shift it occurs, no matter how minor. Notify your steward so we can document it independently. The company cannot discipline a member for filing a legitimate injury report.

Keep your own dated notes; memories fade and paperwork gets lost.
title: Reporting a Workplace Injury

body:
Steward Rights on the Floor

A steward has the right to reasonable paid time to investigate and process grievances. You may request relevant documents from management and interview witnesses. You represent the member, not the company, during any meeting that could lead to discipline.

If a member is called into a meeting that may result in discipline, they have Weingarten rights to union representation.
title: Steward Rights on the Floor

body:
Weingarten Rights Explained

Under Weingarten, a member who reasonably believes an investigatory interview may lead to discipline can request a union representative be present. The employer may proceed without the rep only if it ends the interview. Coach members to say: I request that my steward be present.

The right must be asserted by the employee; the company is not required to offer it.
title: Weingarten Rights Explained

body:
Article 7 — Seniority Summary

Plant seniority begins on the first day worked after the 90-day probationary period and governs layoff, recall, shift preference, and vacation scheduling. Seniority is broken only by quit, discharge for cause, or layoff exceeding the recall period in Section 7.4.

Posted seniority lists must be updated quarterly and the union gets a copy.
title: Article 7 — Seniority Summary

body:
Article 12 — Overtime Equalization

Overtime is offered to qualified employees within the classification in order of low hours on the equalization chart. An employee passed over out of order is owed the missed hours as a remedy, not the chance to work them later. Charts reset annually each January.

Stewards should audit the OT chart monthly and flag discrepancies in writing.
title: Article 12 — Overtime Equalization

body:
Membership Meeting Minutes — January

Meeting called to order at 5:30 PM by President Greene. Quorum present. Treasurer reported the strike fund balance at 142,000 dollars. Bargaining committee gave an update on survey collection. Motion to approve 500 dollars for the food bank drive passed 38 to 2.

Next meeting set for the second Wednesday of February. Adjourned 6:45 PM.
title: Membership Meeting Minutes — January

body:
Executive Board Minutes — February

Board reviewed three pending Step 3 grievances and authorized arbitration on the overtime bypass case. Approved purchase of new steward training binders. Discussed low turnout at general meetings and agreed to add a text-blast reminder system.

Vice President Bell to draft the communications plan for next session.
title: Executive Board Minutes — February

body:
New Shop Organizing Drive — Eastside

We have signed authorization cards from a majority at the Eastside parts depot. Volunteer organizers are mapping the workforce and identifying natural leaders on each shift. House visits are scheduled for the next two weekends.

Keep all card data confidential and stored only with the organizing committee.
title: New Shop Organizing Drive — Eastside

body:
Dues Structure and Check-Off

Monthly dues are 2.0 times the member's hourly rate, deducted automatically through payroll check-off authorization. Initiation fee is a one-time 50 dollars for new hires. Members on layoff or approved leave are placed in honorable withdrawal and pay no dues until recalled.

Questions on a dues deduction error go to the financial secretary.
title: Dues Structure and Check-Off

body:
Job Bidding and Bumping Rules

Posted openings are awarded to the senior qualified bidder. A laid-off employee may bump the least senior employee in an equal or lower classification they are qualified to perform. The displaced member then exercises their own bumping rights down the chain.

Bids must be submitted on the official form within the 5-day posting window.
title: Job Bidding and Bumping Rules

body:
Mandatory Overtime Dispute Guidance

Management may require overtime under Article 12, but only after the voluntary list is exhausted and with proper notice. Members with documented medical or dependent-care conflicts should notify their steward in advance. Refusing a properly assigned mandatory shift can be disciplined, so file a grievance under protest rather than walk off.

Work now, grieve later, unless the assignment is unsafe.
title: Mandatory Overtime Dispute Guidance

body:
Just Cause and the Seven Tests

Discipline must meet just cause: adequate notice of the rule, a reasonable rule related to operations, a fair investigation, proof, even-handed enforcement, and a penalty proportional to the offense. Progressive discipline normally moves from verbal warning to written to suspension to discharge.

Document any inconsistent treatment of other members for the same conduct.
title: Just Cause and the Seven Tests

body:
Shawn's Working Notes — Open Items

Personal tracker: follow up with Tanya on the bargaining survey raw data, confirm arbitration date for the OT bypass case, and rewrite the new-member orientation packet before the next intake class. Draft the safety committee agenda by Friday.

Reminder to back up the grievance log spreadsheet weekly.
title: Shawn's Working Notes — Open Items
